behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR
behaviors.justification: Staleness detection must query real git state (commit ancestry, timestamps) and PR status. Pure unit tests with mocked data could pass while real integration fails due to edge cases in parsing commit output, handling merge commits, or timezone logic. The failure boundary crosses from pure logic into git tool interaction and state comparison.
behaviors.test_description: Set up a test git repository with multiple branches: one stale (commits not in main, timestamp older than threshold, no PR), one clean (no new commits), one recent (commits but too new to be stale), one with merged PR. Verify staleness detection returns correct boolean for each. Verify it correctly parses commit timestamps and compares against configured threshold.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale
behaviors.justification: Requires wiring together current branch detection, staleness check, and warning emission into the session bootstrap path. Unit-level mocking of staleness would not catch integration failures: warning system not triggered, context not passed correctly, output not formatted. Must test the full bootstrap sequence with a stale current branch.
behaviors.test_description: Create a git repo, checkout a stale branch, invoke session bootstrap. Capture warning output. Verify warning contains branch name, age since commit, and actionable suggestion (e.g., 'create a PR for this branch'). Test both stale and clean branches to confirm warning only emitted in stale case.

behaviors.description: /kaizen-wip output distinguishes 'clean' from 'has commits but no PR (STALE)'
behaviors.justification: Tests an endpoint or CLI command that aggregates branch state and formats output. Mocking individual components at unit level misses formatting failures, endpoint wiring, and how results are presented. Must invoke the endpoint and verify output structure correctly distinguishes branch states.
behaviors.test_description: Create a test repository with a mix of branch states: clean (no new commits), stale (commits not in main, no PR), normal-with-PR (commits with open PR), merged-PR (previously had PR, now merged). Call `/kaizen-wip` endpoint (HTTP or CLI). Verify output clearly labels stale branches with 'STALE' or equivalent marker. Verify clean branches are not marked stale and normal branches with PRs are not marked stale.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives
behaviors.justification: Tests staleness detection's PR state logic. Mocking PR queries could hide incorrect state detection (treating merged PRs as stale, or treating open PRs as stale). Must test against real or well-mocked PR state. Depends on implementation: if PR queries are cleanly abstracted, Integration via mocks suffices; if tightly coupled to APIs, may need System.
behaviors.test_description: Create branches in multiple PR states: open PR (should not be stale), merged PR (should not be stale), closed without merge (may be stale), no PR (should be stale if old enough). Verify staleness detection returns false for open and merged PR cases, true for no-PR case. Test both with mocked PR queries (GitHub API mock) and verify correct boolean output.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work
behaviors.justification: This behavior explicitly tests edge cases involving real git operations that change commit hashes (rebase, squash merge). Mocking git to simulate these operations would be complex and error-prone. Must perform actual git operations via subprocess to verify staleness detection correctly adapts to changed branch state. Cannot rely on pure mocks or library calls—need real git behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Create a test repository with a stale branch (commits not in main, old timestamp). Perform a real git rebase on that branch (changing commit hashes). Verify staleness detection no longer identifies it as unPRed stale work (because rebasing integrates work into branch lineage). Test squash merge scenario: create PR, squash-merge branch into main, verify branch no longer flagged as stale. Verify detection adapts correctly when branch state changes due to git operations.
